proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_015 (range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is associated with pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort; specifically, higher feature_015 is associated with longer pfs_months.

Across all 33 candidate features, several binary and continuous features have non-zero univariable associations with pfs_months: at least one of {feature_023, feature_011, feature_016, feature_028, feature_018} has a univariable mean-difference > 0.3 months in absolute value.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; r,p = stats.pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months']); slope = stats.linregress(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months']).slope
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.7571 (p ~0). Linear slope = +0.170 months of PFS per year of feature_015. Mean PFS rises monotonically across age deciles from 0.74 (age <=52) to 6.55 (age >=78).

analyses.code: Univariable t-tests / Pearson tests of every feature_* vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Strongest univariable effects: feature_015 (r=+0.76), feature_014 (3-level, mean 4.33/3.19/2.15), feature_023 (diff=-1.44), feature_011 (-0.92), feature_006 squamous vs adeno (-0.86), feature_016 (+0.78), feature_028 (-0.81), feature_018 (+0.32), feature_017 (r=-0.13), feature_025 (r=+0.10), feature_031 (-0.20). Several binaries are essentially null (feature_012, feature_020, feature_027).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable OLS model of pfs_months on all 33 features (with feature_001 and feature_006 dummy-encoded and feature_014 treated as ordinal), feature_015 (age), feature_014 (3-level severity), feature_023, feature_011, feature_016, feature_018, feature_025, feature_017, feature_006 (squamous), feature_001 (current smoker), and feature_031 are jointly significant predictors of pfs_months while feature_012, feature_020, feature_022, feature_026, feature_007, feature_009, feature_010, feature_013, feature_004, feature_008, feature_027, and feature_030 are non-significant.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; sm.OLS(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted R^2 = 0.894. Significant (p<0.001) covariates and signs match the predicted set: feature_015 (+0.168/yr), feature_014 (-1.15 for level 1, -2.18 for level 2), feature_023 (-1.42), feature_011 (-0.91), feature_016 (+0.89), feature_018 (+0.32), feature_006 squamous (-0.72), feature_001 current (-0.55), feature_017 (-0.073/unit), feature_025 (+0.46/unit), feature_031 (-0.20), feature_032 (-4e-4/unit), feature_005 (-0.11). Predicted nulls confirmed: feature_012 (p=0.38), feature_020 (p=0.71), feature_022 (p=0.94), feature_026 (p=0.98), feature_007 (p=0.61), feature_009 (p=0.68), feature_013 (p=0.31), feature_004 (p=0.75), feature_008 (p=0.40), feature_027 (p=0.87), feature_030 (p=0.48), feature_010 (p=0.93). feature_021 and feature_002 are weakly null/marginal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 is an ordinal severity / stage marker: mean pfs_months declines monotonically as feature_014 goes from 0 to 1 to 2.
Patients with feature_006 = squamous have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_006 = adenocarcinoma.
Among the three feature_001 categories, current smokers have the shortest mean pfs_months and never/former smokers are similar.

analyses.result_summary: Means: feature_014=0 -> 4.33 (n=17557), =1 -> 3.19 (n=24962), =2 -> 2.15 (n=7481). Strict monotonic decline. ANOVA p ~0.
Squamous mean PFS = 2.81 (n=13901) vs adenocarcinoma 3.67 (n=36099); diff = -0.86 months, p ~0.

former 3.61 (n=27567), current 2.99 (n=14974), never 3.68 (n=7459). Current smokers ~0.6 months lower than the other two; former and never are nearly identical (consistent with current-smoker-specific decrement).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_023 = 1 have substantially shorter pfs_months than feature_023 = 0 (a strong adverse prognostic marker).
Patients with feature_011 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_011 = 0.
Patients with feature_028 = 1 have shorter pfs_months than feature_028 = 0 (rare ~5% adverse marker).

analyses.result_summary: feature_023=1 mean PFS 2.93 (n=32365) vs =0 4.36 (n=17635); diff = -1.44 months, p ~0.
feature_011=1 mean PFS 2.74 (n=12584) vs =0 3.66 (n=37416); diff = -0.92 months, p ~0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_016 = 1 have longer mean pfs_months than feature_016 = 0 (a positive marker, ~13% prevalence).
Patients with feature_018 = 1 have modestly longer mean pfs_months than feature_018 = 0 (~35% prevalence).
Binary features feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 have no detectable univariable association with pfs_months (i.e. they are noise variables in this dataset).

analyses.result_summary: feature_012: diff = -0.035, p = 0.081; feature_020: diff = -0.025, p = 0.255; feature_027: diff = +0.004, p = 0.870. All three are univariably null and remain null in the multivariable model. Interpretation: these are noise / non-prognostic indicator variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous laboratory-like features, only feature_017 (range 0-28, plausibly a count/ratio), feature_025 (range 1.7-5.5, plausibly albumin-like), and feature_032 (range 50-829, plausibly LDH/platelet-like) have non-trivial associations with pfs_months; the remaining lab-like continuous features (feature_003, feature_004, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_010, feature_013, feature_026, feature_029, feature_030, feature_033) have negligible univariable associations.

analyses.code: Pearson r vs pfs_months for each continuous variable
analyses.result_summary: Significant lab signals: feature_017 r=-0.127 (p=7.5e-179), feature_025 r=+0.099 (p=2.8e-109), feature_032 r=-0.019 (p=3.6e-5). All other continuous lab-like features have |r|<=0.007 and p>0.1, consistent with no real association. In the multivariable model: feature_017 -0.073/unit (p<1e-87), feature_025 +0.46/unit (p<1e-71), feature_032 -4e-4/unit (p=2e-28). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pairwise correlations among the 13 binary features are essentially zero (|r| < 0.11 for every pair), consistent with features generated independently rather than the structured comorbidity pattern of a typical EHR cohort.
feature_022 is differentially distributed across smoking categories (feature_001): it is enriched in never-smokers compared with current smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Largest absolute pairwise binary correlation = 0.103 (feature_022, feature_021). Almost all pairs |r|<0.01. Confirmed.

Prevalence of feature_022=1: 51% in never-smokers, 8% in former, 3% in current. Strong enrichment in never-smokers (chi-square p ~0). However, feature_022 has no detectable main effect on pfs_months (multivariable p=0.94) and no notable subgroup-specific effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A pairwise treatment-effect-modification screen across all 13 binary features (each pair fitted as y ~ a + b + a*b) will identify at least one strongly significant interaction; the largest will involve feature_016 and feature_018.

analyses.result_summary: Top interactions (coef, p): feature_016*feature_018 +2.558 (p ~0); feature_031*feature_016 -1.694 (p=3e-178); feature_031*feature_018 -0.562 (p=5e-41); feature_016*feature_028 -1.210 (p=3e-16); feature_028*feature_018 -0.488 (p=4e-7); feature_016*feature_005 -0.817 (p=3e-6); feature_022*feature_018 -0.241 (p=1e-4). The dominant signal is feature_016 x feature_018 as predicted; the next-strongest interactions all involve feature_016 or feature_018 paired with feature_031, feature_028, or feature_005 (potential effect modifiers).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying by feature_016 and feature_018, the PFS benefit of feature_018 (treatment-like indicator) is concentrated in feature_016 = 1 patients: among feature_016=0 the difference between feature_018=1 and feature_018=0 is essentially zero, while among feature_016=1 the difference exceeds +2 months.

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: (f016=0,f018=0)=3.334 (n=28334); (f016=0,f018=1)=3.329 (n=15298); (f016=1,f018=0)=3.213 (n=4122); (f016=1,f018=1)=5.766 (n=2246). Effect of feature_018: -0.005 in feature_016=0 (null), +2.553 in feature_016=1 (huge, p<1e-200). The marginal +0.32 of feature_018 is entirely driven by the f016=1 stratum. Symmetrically, feature_016 has -0.12 effect when feature_018=0 and +2.44 when feature_018=1. Hypothesis fully supported. Working interpretation: feature_018 is a treatment indicator and feature_016 is its predictive biomarker (or vice versa).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (feature_016=1 AND feature_018=1) responder subgroup, the treatment benefit is abolished by feature_031=1: the (f016=1, f018=1, f031=0) cell has mean PFS roughly twice that of the (f016=1, f018=1, f031=1) cell, while the f031 main effect outside the responder cell is essentially zero.

analyses.result_summary: 8-cell predictions: (f018=0,f016=0,f031=0)=3.334; (f018=0,f016=0,f031=1)=3.334; (f018=0,f016=1,f031=0)=3.232; (f018=0,f016=1,f031=1)=3.191; (f018=1,f016=0,f031=0)=3.315; (f018=1,f016=0,f031=1)=3.346; (f018=1,f016=1,f031=0)=7.876 (n=1237); (f018=1,f016=1,f031=1)=3.179 (n=1009). Three-way interaction coefficient feature_018:feature_016:feature_031 = -4.688 (p~0). Outside the responder cell the f031 effect is ~0 (e.g. (f016=0,f018=1) cells differ by 0.031). Hypothesis confirmed: feature_031 is a near-pure treatment-effect modifier with no main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the (feature_018=1 AND feature_016=1) responder subgroup, additional treatment-response suppressors beyond feature_031 will be identifiable; specifically, feature_028=1 and feature_005=1 will each be associated with substantially lower PFS in this subgroup compared to feature_028=0 / feature_005=0.

analyses.result_summary: Within core (n=2246, mean PFS 5.77): feature_031=0 mean=7.88 vs =1 mean=3.18 (loss 4.70); feature_028=0 mean=5.91 vs =1 mean=2.08 (loss 3.83, n=87); feature_005=0 mean=5.84 vs =1 mean=3.36 (loss 2.48, n=66); feature_023=0 mean=6.71 vs =1 mean=5.24 (loss 1.47, but feature_023 has a strong main prognostic effect everywhere); feature_011=0 mean=6.04 vs =1 mean=4.93. The three largest pure suppressors are feature_031, feature_028, feature_005; feature_023 and feature_011 are general prognostic markers, not treatment-effect-specific. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_031 and feature_005 act as pure treatment-effect modifiers: outside the (feature_018=1 AND feature_016=1) responder cell, neither has a meaningful main effect on pfs_months (mean differences |diff| < 0.05), while inside the responder cell each has a large negative effect.

feature_028 is a hybrid factor: it has a small adverse main effect on pfs_months across the whole cohort (-0.67 outside the responder cell), and an additional, larger suppressive effect inside the responder cell.

analyses.code: Outside core: mean pfs by feature_031 / feature_005
analyses.result_summary: Outside the (f018=1, f016=1) cell (n=47754): feature_031=1 mean PFS = 3.325 vs =0 = 3.319 (diff = +0.006); feature_005=1 mean = 3.315 vs =0 = 3.322 (diff = -0.007). Both effects indistinguishable from zero. Inside the cell: feature_031 reduces PFS by 4.70, feature_005 by 2.48. Hypothesis supported: these are pure modifiers.

Outside the responder cell, feature_028=1 mean PFS = 2.682 vs =0 = 3.355 (diff = -0.673, n=2377 vs 45377). Inside responder cell, additional drop of about -3.83. Confirms hybrid behavior: small main effect plus stronger interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full responder subgroup definition is feature_018=1 AND feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0. In this subgroup, mean pfs_months exceeds 8 months and is more than 4.9 months longer than the matched biomarker-positive untreated counterfactual (feature_018=0 AND feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0).

analyses.code: ideal = df.query('feature_018==1 & feature_016==1 & feature_031==0 & feature_028==0 & feature_005==0'); ctrl same but feature_018==0
analyses.result_summary: Ideal subgroup: n=1154, mean PFS = 8.234. Counterfactual (treatment-eligible biomarker-positive without the treatment indicator): n=2112, mean PFS = 3.249. Mean difference = +4.985 months, two-sample t-test p ~0. Within the (f018=1 & f016=1) cell, the eight sub-cells defined by (f031, f028, f005) give: (0,0,0)=8.23 (n=1154), (0,0,1)=3.57 (n=37), (0,1,0)=2.44 (n=44), (1,0,0)=3.23 (n=944), (1,0,1)=3.41 (n=24), (1,1,0)=1.75 (n=38). The treatment-benefit cell is sharp and well-defined.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression-tree model fitted only inside the (feature_018=1 AND feature_016=1) responder subgroup will rediscover feature_031 (and secondarily feature_028) as the dominant top-of-tree splits, confirming the subgroup-discovery results were not artifacts of the manual interaction screen.

analyses.result_summary: Top split at root: feature_031 <= 0.5 (improvement in PFS variance). On the f031=0 branch, second split is age (feature_015) and then feature_028; the f031=0 / older / feature_028=0 leaves have predicted PFS 7.0-11.6, whereas the f031=0 / feature_028=1 leaf is 1.35. On the f031=1 branch, leaf predictions are 0.6-6.85, mostly far below the f031=0 leaves. Tree structure confirms feature_031 (then feature_028) as dominant treatment modifiers, with age (feature_015) as a continuous secondary modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature in the dataset acts as a treatment with heterogeneous effect comparable to feature_018: in particular, feature_021, feature_022, feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 do not show a subgroup with > 1-month treatment effect at any 2-feature stratification.

Final, best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months: feature_018 = 1 (a treatment indicator) extends progression-free survival by approximately +5 months exclusively in the subgroup defined by feature_016 = 1 AND feature_031 = 0 AND feature_028 = 0 AND feature_005 = 0; in every other subgroup the effect of feature_018 on pfs_months is essentially zero. Equivalently, the treatment 'works' only when the biomarker feature_016 is positive and none of the three resistance markers (feature_031, feature_028, feature_005) are present.

analyses.code: for each candidate tx in {f021,f022,f002,f012,f020,f027}: stratify by every other binary; compute (tx=1 - tx=0) within each (other=v) cell
analyses.result_summary: feature_021: effect ranges from -0.41 (f028=1 stratum) to +0.01 (f005=1 stratum); never exceeds 0.5 in absolute value in any stratum. feature_022: largest stratum-specific effect was +0.46 (early stage f014=0) vs +0.06 (advanced f014=2); no >1-month subgroup effect. feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 are uniformly within +/-0.2 across all strata. None mimic the feature_018/feature_016 pattern.

analyses.code: Effect of feature_018 in the four cells defined by feature_016 x (any of feature_031, feature_028, feature_005)
analyses.result_summary: Within (feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0): feature_018=1 mean PFS 8.234 (n=1154) vs feature_018=0 mean 3.249 (n=2112); diff = +4.99 months, p ~0. Within the negation of any one of the four predicates: feature_018 effect is between -0.2 and +0.2. The full subgroup-specific treatment effect is therefore: TE(feature_018=1 vs 0 | feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0) = +4.99 months (95% CI roughly +4.8 to +5.2 from the pooled SE); TE elsewhere ~0. This is the final subgroup definition.
